error[E0277]: the trait bound `&mut f32: Copy` is not satisfied
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-10T00-24-35/mistral-codestral-2508/declarations/array_decl_1/sample_001/target.rs:14:20
   |
14 |     let mut afp = [&mut 0.0f32; 17];
   |                    ^^^^^^^^^^^ the trait `Copy` is not implemented for `&mut f32`
   |
   = note: `Copy` is implemented for `&f32`, but not for `&mut f32`
   = note: the `Copy` trait is required because this value will be copied for each element of the array
   = help: consider using `core::array::from_fn` to initialize the array
   = help: see https://doc.rust-lang.org/stable/std/array/fn.from_fn.html for more information
help: consider removing the leading `&`-reference

14 +     let mut afp = [0.0f32; 17];

error[E0506]: cannot assign to `backing[_]` because it is borrowed
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-10T00-24-35/mistral-codestral-2508/declarations/array_decl_1/sample_001/target.rs:22:13

17 |     fn init_globals(fa: &mut [f32; 11], afp: &mut [&mut f32; 17], backing: &mut [f32; 17]) {
   |                                                    - let's call the lifetime of this reference `'2`
...
22 |             backing[i] = (100 + i) as f32;
   |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ `backing[_]` is assigned to here but it was already borrowed
23 |             afp[i] = &mut backing[i];
   |             ------------------------
   |             |        |
   |             |        `backing[_]` is borrowed here
   |             assignment requires that `backing[_]` is borrowed for `'2`
